metadata.career_relevance: Palindrome problems appear in 30% of Zerodha/Razorpay string rounds. 'Longest palindromic substring' is asked at every FAANG India interview at some point.

hook.real_world_problem:
```
Zerodha stock ticker validation:
Is 'AMAAMA' a palindrome ticker? Yes.
Is 'RELIANCE' symmetric? No.

Expand around center:
'A M A A M A'
      ↑↑ ← center between 3rd A and A
Expand: 'AAMAAMA'? No — it's 'AMAAMA' = palindrome!
O(n) vs O(n²) brute force
```
hook.what_you_will_build: Palindrome checker, longest palindromic substring (expand around center), and valid palindrome after one deletion.

visual_aid.description: Expand around center diagram on 'babad'. Show 5 odd centers: b(0), a(1), b(2), a(3), d(4). Center 1 (a): expand → b≠d, stop → palindrome 'a' len 1. Center 2 (b): expand left a, right a → a==a → continue → b,a == a,b? → no → palindrome 'aba' len 3. Also check even centers. Final: longest = 'bab' or 'aba' len 3.
visual_aid.alt_text: Expand around center for each of 2n-1 positions

worked_example.problem_statement: Return any one longest palindromic substring. 'babad'→'bab' or 'aba'.

step_by_step_solution.details: 5 odd centers + 4 even centers = 9 total expansions. O(n) per expansion worst case.

worked_example.final_code: String res=""; for(int i=0;i<n;i++){String odd=expand(s,i,i);String even=expand(s,i,i+1);String best=odd.length()>even.length()?odd:even;if(best.length()>res.length())res=best;} return res;
things_to_notice: Each expansion is O(n) worst case → total O(n²)
Manacher's algorithm achieves O(n) — usually overkill for interviews

industry_examples.use_case: Validate palindromic stock tickers in O(1) extra space

applications.interview_relevance: 'Is this string a palindrome?' → immediate O(1) space two-pointer. 'Longest palindromic substring' → expand around center O(n²). Mention Manacher's for bonus points.
applications.real_world_workflow: Palindrome recognition: two-pointer → O(1) space. Longest palindromic substring: expand around center → O(n²) time O(1) space. Palindromic subsequence (not substring): DP.

common_gaps.what_students_get_wrong: Only checking odd-length palindromes. 'abba' has center between 'b' and 'b' (even-length). Must check both odd and even centers.
common_gaps.remediation: For each index i: (1) expand around (i, i) — odd length, (2) expand around (i, i+1) — even length. Always both.
common_gaps.detection_pattern: Student returns 'a' for 'abba' (single char palindrome) instead of 'abba'

key_takeaways: Two-pointer palindrome check: O(n) time O(1) space
2n-1 centers: n odd + n-1 even — always check both
Expand around center: O(n²) total — good enough for interviews
Manacher's O(n) — know the concept, rarely need to implement
Valid Palindrome II: try deleting one char from either side on mismatch

self_check_questions.question: How many centers are there for a string of length n?
self_check_questions.answer: 2n-1 centers total. n centers for odd-length palindromes (single characters as centers: indices 0 through n-1). n-1 centers for even-length palindromes (gaps between adjacent characters: between indices 0-1, 1-2, ..., (n-2)-(n-1)). So total = n + (n-1) = 2n-1. Example: 'abba' has 4 odd centers (a,b,b,a) and 3 even centers (between each pair). The even center between the two b's gives the palindrome 'abba'.

reflection.next_topic_preview: Next: Anagram & Frequency — solve all anagram problems using frequency arrays and sliding windows.
